page_content:
ARTT255 Introduction to Digital Art and Design Processes (3 Credits) Introduction to basic software and principles of digital imaging, and how they are applied to art and design. Topics covered: Digital image construction and manipulation, Vector-Based digital techniques layout, typography, etc), time-based digital techniques (video and audio composition and manipulation), and basic interactivity (web-design). Digital media used to explore visual principles established in ARTT100. Prerequisite: ARTT100 and ARTT110. Credit Only Granted for: ARTT255 or ARTT354. Formerly: ARTT354.

ARTT260 Dangerous Art: Censorship or Subsidy (3 Credits) Combines a broad historical analysis of the relationship between art and authority with an examination of contemporary culture criticism and art practice. Explores the uses and abuses of art and culture in totalitarianand theocratic states as a prelude to a review of the role of ofﬁcial culture in the United States. Examines art and culture in the public arena and many related areas where the arts and policy interact.

ARTH498 Directed Studies in Art History I (2-3 Credits) Prerequisite: Permission of ARHU-Art History & Archaeology department. Restriction: Junior standing or higher. Repeatable to: 99 credits if content differs.
